faculty_name: College of Basic and Applied Sciences
note: CBAS comprises the School of Engineering Sciences, School of Physical & Mathematical Sciences, School of Biological Sciences, School of Agriculture, and School of Veterinary Medicine.
Undergraduate regular fees (2025/26): Science GHC 3,462/sem; Agriculture GHC 3,544/sem; Applied Sciences/Vet GHC 3,621/sem. Fee-paying: GHC 7,405-9,152/sem.
Postgraduate MPhil Year 1 (1st tier): GHC 14,911 (Sciences/Agric) | GHC 13,365 (Applied Sciences). PhD full-time Year 1: GHC 12,093.

All undergraduate applicants need credits in English, Core Mathematics, Integrated Science, and three science electives with total aggregate not exceeding 24 (Regular) or as programme-specific cut-offs.

fees: GHC 3,621 per semester (Regular) | GHC 9,152 per semester (Fee-paying)
mode: Full-time on-campus with engineering labs and farm practicals
Field-based project work and industry attachment
entry_requirements: Credits (A1-C6) in English, Core Mathematics, Integrated Science, and three science electives
B3 or better in Elective Mathematics required
Aggregate not exceeding 15 (Regular) / 19-20 (Fee-Paying)
level_note: Agricultural Engineering applies engineering principles to farming systems, food processing, and rural infrastructure.
Graduates work with MoFA, agro-processing firms, FAO, and international development agencies.

fees: GHC 3,621 per semester (Regular) | GHC 9,152 per semester (Fee-paying)

Aggregate not exceeding 6 (Regular) / 8 (Fee-Paying)
level_note: BSc. Biomedical Engineering is one of the most competitive UG programmes with a cut-off of just 6.
Graduates serve in teaching hospitals, medical device companies, and biomedical research centres.

note: CHS comprises the School of Medicine and Dentistry, School of Pharmacy, School of Nursing, School of Biomedical and Allied Health Sciences (SBAHS), and the School of Public Health.
All CHS programmes except those in SBAHS must be selected as FIRST CHOICE only — second-choice applications are not accepted.

Undergraduate regular fees (2025/26): Medical School GHC 3,376-3,959/yr; Dental/Pharmacy GHC 3,366-4,937/yr; SBAHS GHC 4,005-4,676/yr; Nursing GHC 3,955-4,249/yr; SPH (fee-paying only) GHC 8,635-9,604/yr.
Postgraduate fees (2025/26): 1-yr MSc (1st tier) GHC 13,842; MPhil Yr1 (1st tier) GHC 13,877; PhD Yr1 GHC 12,145; MPH Weekend GHC 19,258/yr.
All health sciences graduates must complete mandatory internship/housemanship under GHS or the relevant professional regulatory body before independent practice.

courses.course_name: Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery (MB ChB)

fees: Regular Yr1 (2025/26): GHC 3,376/yr | Yr2: GHC 3,959/yr | Clinical yrs: GHC 3,594/yr

level_note: MB ChB at UG School of Medicine is Ghana's most prestigious medical degree with an aggregate of 8.
Graduates complete one-year mandatory housemanship before registration with the Medical and Dental Council of Ghana.

level_note: BDS is offered by UG School of Dentistry at Korle-Bu, the only public dental school in Ghana.
Graduates complete an internship year and register with the Medical and Dental Council of Ghana.

note: COH comprises the School of Arts, School of Social Sciences, University of Ghana School of Law (UGSL), and the School of Languages.
Undergraduate regular fees (2025/26): Humanities BA GHC 2,698/sem; Admin/Law GHC 2,814/sem; Fee-paying BA GHC 5,674/sem; Admin/Law GHC 6,757/sem; Post-1st-Degree LLB GHC 14,443/yr.
Postgraduate special law programmes (LLM/MA): GHC 22,884/yr. MPhil/two-year MA (1st tier): GHC 13,950 Yr1, GHC 13,125 Yr2. PhD Humanities: GHC 10,655 Yr1. PhD Admin/Science: GHC 12,093 Yr1.
UGBS Special PG (MBA/EMBA/MSc/MA) fees (2025/26): EMBA/MBA GHC 12,062/sem; MSc Weekend GHC 16,255/sem; MA Weekend GHC 13,299/sem.

fees: GHC 2,814 per semester (Regular Admin/Law) | GHC 6,757 (Fee-paying)

Post-1st-Degree LLB: any accredited first degree plus entrance examination
level_note: LLB at UG School of Law is the most competitive non-science undergraduate programme, requiring aggregate 7.

note: The College of Education (COE) trains teachers and educational leaders for pre-tertiary and higher education across Ghana, delivered on-campus and via distance/blended modes through SCDE.
Undergraduate regular fees (2025/26): BA Education freshmen GHC 3,110/sem; BSc Education freshmen GHC 3,874/sem; Fee-paying BA GHC 6,086/sem; Fee-paying BSc GHC 7,817/sem.
Distance Education (SCDE) fees: BA freshmen GHC 4,859/sem; BSc/Admin freshmen GHC 5,091/sem. Distance Diploma freshmen: GHC 4,813-4,937/sem.
Postgraduate: MA Education (2nd tier, weekend/evening) GHC 12,027/yr; MA Strategic Communication GHC 26,567/yr; MPhil Educational Technology GHC 18,729 Yr1.
All Bachelor of Education programmes have a WASSCE cut-off of Aggregate 24 except BEd ICT (Aggregate 12/15) and BEd Computer Science (Aggregate 9/12).

courses.course_name: Bachelor of Education — Social Studies / English / French / Spanish / Arabic / History / Geography / Religious Studies / Physical Education & Health

fees: GHC 3,110 freshmen / GHC 2,253 continuing (Regular BA) | GHC 6,086 freshmen (Fee-paying)
cutt_off: Relevant subject credit required (e.g. C6 in French/Spanish/Arabic/Literature as applicable)
mode: Full-time on-campus with school-based teaching practicum
Subject-specific pedagogy and curriculum modules
entry_requirements: Credits (A1-C6) in English, Core Mathematics, Social Studies, and three relevant arts/languages electives
Aggregate not exceeding 24; C6 in target language/subject where applicable
level_note: These BEd programmes train specialist teachers for Social Studies, English, French, Spanish, Arabic, and related arts/languages subjects at SHS level.
Graduates qualify for GES posting and may pursue MA Education or language-specialist postgraduate degrees.

courses.course_name: Bachelor of Education — Mathematics / Science (Physics / Chemistry / Biology) / Consumer Sciences

fees: GHC 3,874 freshmen / GHC 3,017 continuing (Regular BSc) | GHC 7,817 freshmen (Fee-paying)
cutt_off: Required subject credits: C6 in Elective Maths (BEd Maths); C6 in relevant science (BEd Physics/Chemistry/Biology); C6 in Biology, Chemistry, and Management in Living (Consumer Sciences)
mode: Full-time on-campus with science/maths pedagogy, laboratory practicals, and teaching practicum
Research project in subject-specific education
entry_requirements: Credits (A1-C6) in English, Core Mathematics, Integrated Science, and three science electives including required subjects
Aggregate not exceeding 24 for STEM routes; 20-22 for Consumer Sciences
level_note: BEd Mathematics and Science programmes train specialist teachers in Ghana's critical STEM subject shortage areas.
BEd Consumer Sciences trains specialist Home Economics, Food & Nutrition, and Textiles teachers for Ghana's SHS curriculum.

courses.course_name: Bachelor of Education — ICT / Computer Science

fees: GHC 3,874 freshmen / GHC 3,017 continuing (Regular BSc) | GHC 7,817 freshmen (Fee-paying)
cutt_off: 2025/26: BEd ICT — Aggregate 12 (Regular) / 15 (Fee-Paying); C4 or better in Core Mathematics required
2025/26: BEd Computer Science — Aggregate 9 (Regular) / 12 (Fee-Paying); C4 in Elective and Core Mathematics required
mode: Full-time on-campus with computing labs and teaching practicum
E-learning and educational technology practicals
entry_requirements: Credits (A1-C6) in English, Core Mathematics, Social Studies, and three relevant electives
BEd ICT: C4 in Core Mathematics; BEd CS: C4 in Elective and Core Mathematics
level_note: BEd ICT and BEd Computer Science are among the most competitive education degrees at COE, reflecting Ghana's critical digital skills teacher shortage.
Graduates are in high demand in GES schools, private schools, and educational technology companies.

courses.course_name: Bachelor of Education — Early Grade / JHS / Upper Grade Specialism / Sports and Physical Culture / Performing Arts (Music / Dance / Theatre Arts)

Graduates are posted by GES and may pursue distance MA Education for career advancement.
